serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving is one piece of you tiao with fish paste, weighing approximately 60 g, commonly consumed as a breakfast snack or light meal accompaniment in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: You Tiao with Fish Paste — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for 'You Tiao with Fish Paste' (cr_id F11090070). All core macros and secondary nutrients are sourced from this canonical HPB record with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat, monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, cholesterol, potassium, vitamins, and other micronutrients not reported in the HPB entry are set to -1 (data not available).

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal); University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for you tiao with fish paste. GI estimated at 75 (high) based on the analogous food 'Chinese fried dough (you tiao / cruller)', which is composed predominantly of refined wheat flour and deep-fried, consistent with GI values of 74–76 reported for similar deep-fried refined-flour dough products in the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care 31:2281–2283). Fish paste component is a minor protein addition that does not materially lower the GI of the refined-flour base. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI (75) × carbohydrate per serving (60 g × 0.331 = 19.86 g) / 100 = 14.9.

hypertension.key_factor: Moderate sodium (192 mg/100 g) combined with high fat from deep-frying
hypertension.guidance: Sodium at 192 mg/100 g is below the 200 mg threshold, but the high fat content (19.6 g/100 g) from deep-frying contributes to caloric density and cardiovascular load; individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to one piece and avoid pairing with other high-sodium foods.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High-GI refined wheat flour base (GI ~75) with low dietary fibre (2.0 g/100 g)
type2_diabetes.guidance: The refined wheat flour base produces a high glycaemic response (estimated GI ~75); individuals with type 2 diabetes should limit consumption to half a piece, pair with non-starchy vegetables and additional protein, and avoid eating this food on an empty stomach to blunt the postprandial glucose spike.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High total fat (19.6 g/100 g) from deep-frying with estimated saturated fat and potential trans fat from frying oil
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Deep-frying in vegetable oil contributes significant total fat (19.6 g/100 g) and estimated saturated fat (~3.5 g/100 g); individuals with cardiovascular disease should restrict intake to occasional consumption, choose baked or air-fried alternatives where available, and monitor cumulative saturated fat intake across the day.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Deep-frying generates advanced glycation end-products (AGEs) and potential trans fat — both pro-inflammatory
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Deep-fried preparation is a hard disqualifier for rheumatoid arthritis management due to AGE formation and likely trace trans fat from frying oil; individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this food or request baked or air-fried preparation.

llm_training.natural_language_description: You Tiao with Fish Paste (油条配鱼浆) is a Singaporean variant of the classic Chinese deep-fried dough fritter (you tiao / youtiao), enriched with a filling or coating of fish paste or octopus paste. The base dough is made from refined wheat flour leavened with baking powder, then deep-fried in vegetable oil until golden and crispy on the outside with a light, airy interior. The addition of fish paste introduces a savoury, umami-rich flavour and a slightly denser, chewier texture compared to plain you tiao. This snack is commonly sold at hawker centres and kopitiams across Singapore, typically consumed as a breakfast item or mid-morning snack, often alongside soy milk, congee, or kaya toast. Nutritionally, each 100 g provides 341 kcal, 8.3 g protein, 19.6 g fat, and 33.1 g carbohydrate, with modest dietary fibre at 2.0 g and sodium at 192 mg. The high fat content reflects the deep-frying method. The refined flour base gives this food a high estimated glycaemic index (~75), making it a concern for individuals managing type 2 diabetes or cardiovascular disease. The fish paste component adds protein and some micronutrients but does not meaningfully lower the glycaemic response. Allergens include gluten and fish. This food is generally considered halal and dairy-free.

confidence_reason: Core macros and secondary nutrients sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo canonical database entry for this exact food item (cr_id F11090070) with confidence 0.95. GI is an analogous estimate based on approved University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed literature for deep-fried refined-flour dough products; this is documented. Saturated fat estimated from USDA FoodData Central data for comparable deep-fried dough products as the HPB entry does not report fatty acid breakdown.
notes: 1. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, cholesterol_mg, potassium_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, cholesterol, potassium, vitamins, and moisture data for this composite fried dough with fish paste are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry (F11090070) as of 2026-04-16, and no equivalent entry was found in USDA FoodData Central for this exact composite. 2. saturated_fat_g estimated at 3.5 g/100 g based on USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for 'Bread, fried, Indian (Navajo)' and comparable deep-fried wheat dough products; this is an approximation and should be verified against a direct laboratory analysis. 3. GI of 75 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for you tiao with fish paste was found in the University of Sydney GI Database or peer-reviewed literature as of 2026-04-16. 4. The input record listed 'rheumatoid_arthritis' as a health suitability condition; this has been preserved in additional_conditions. 5. Halal status assumed based on typical hawker preparation; individual vendors may vary.
